name: HCF and LCM — Common Factors and Multiples
whatIsIt: The HCF (Highest Common Factor) is the BIGGEST number that divides two numbers exactly — great for splitting things into the largest equal groups. The LCM (Lowest Common Multiple) is the SMALLEST number both go into — great for when two cycles line up again. HCF looks at shared FACTORS; LCM looks at shared MULTIPLES.
whyNeeded: HCF and LCM solve real timing and grouping problems — when two bells ring together again, arranging tiles with no cuts, or simplifying fractions to lowest terms. They're built on factors, multiples and primes.
realLifeUses: Two bells ring every 4 and 6 minutes — when do they ring together? (LCM)
Cutting two ribbons of 12 m and 18 m into equal biggest pieces (HCF)
Simplifying a fraction to lowest terms (divide by the HCF)
Packing two item counts into equal boxes with none left over

vocabulary.meaning: The highest number that is a factor of both numbers.
The lowest number that is a multiple of both numbers.
Shared by both numbers (a common factor or common multiple).

story.title: The Two Temple Bells
story.text: One bell rings every 4 minutes, another every 6. They just rang together. 'When will they ring together again?' asks Dada. Priya lists multiples: 4, 8, 12… and 6, 12, 18… 'The first shared one is 12!' The LCM of 4 and 6 is 12 — 'so they ring together every 12 minutes.'
story.extractedProblem: Bells ring every 4 and 6 minutes. After how many minutes do they ring together again?

visual.caption: First shared multiple of 4 and 6 is 12 → LCM = 12. The bells meet at 12.

teachingGallery.title: Factors for HCF, multiples for LCM
teachingGallery.note: HCF = biggest shared factor; LCM = smallest shared multiple.

examples.caption: Biggest common factor of 12 & 18 is 6 → HCF = 6.
HCF makes the largest equal groups.

standardMethod.summary: For HCF, list factors of both and pick the biggest shared one. For LCM, list multiples of both and pick the smallest shared one.
steps: HCF: list the factors of each number; find the ones they share; take the HIGHEST.
LCM: list the multiples of each; find the first they share; take the LOWEST.
Shortcut with primes: HCF = product of shared prime factors; LCM = HCF × the leftover parts.
Check: the HCF divides both; both divide the LCM.

commonMistakes.mistake: Mixing up HCF and LCM — giving the smallest common factor or the largest multiple.
commonMistakes.fix: HCF is the HIGHEST common FACTOR (a divisor); LCM is the LOWEST common MULTIPLE (a bigger number).

commonMistakes.mistake: Picking a factor or multiple of only ONE number, not shared by both.
commonMistakes.fix: It must be COMMON — appear in both lists. Check it works for both numbers.

easy.q: What is the HCF of 6 and 9?
What is the LCM of 2 and 3?

medium.q: What is the HCF of 8 and 12?
What is the LCM of 4 and 5?

challenge.q: Two lights blink every 6 and 8 seconds. After how many seconds do they blink together?
hintLadder: This is the LCM of 6 and 8.

challenge.q: For the LARGEST equal pieces from ribbons of 12 m and 18 m, you need…
hintLadder: Biggest equal cut = highest common factor.

questions.q: When two cycles next line up together, you use the…

teachBackPrompt: Pick two small numbers (like 6 and 8). Show a grown-up how to find their HCF (biggest shared factor) and LCM (smallest shared multiple), and give a real example for each — cutting equal pieces (HCF) and two bells meeting (LCM).

revisionCard.summary: HCF = Highest Common FACTOR (biggest divisor of both) → largest equal groups, simplify fractions. LCM = Lowest Common MULTIPLE (smallest number both go into) → when cycles meet again. Both must be COMMON to the two numbers.

rememberIt.hook: HCF = biggest shared factor. LCM = smallest shared multiple
rememberIt.unpack: Factors are small, so HCF is at most your numbers. Multiples are big, so LCM is at least your numbers. Handy link: HCF x LCM = the two numbers multiplied.

studentQuestions.q: How do I stop mixing up HCF and LCM?
studentQuestions.a: HCF = Highest Common FACTOR - factors are small, so HCF is at most your numbers. LCM = Lowest Common MULTIPLE - multiples are big, so LCM is at least your numbers.

studentQuestions.q: When do I use each one?
studentQuestions.a: HCF: splitting things into the biggest equal groups. LCM: when events repeat together - bells ringing, or buses arriving at the same time.

formulas.name: HCF-LCM link
formulas.formula: HCF x LCM = a x b
formulas.remember: Their product equals the product of the two numbers
formulas.whenToUse: Find one when you know the other and both numbers.
